claim.content: The numerical comparison that states the computed flat-band bandwidths for bilayer graphene under the polar h-BN superlattice (with narrowest manifolds on the order of 5–10 meV) are comparable to the bandwidths reported for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (also quoted as about 5–10 meV) is assumed to be a meaningful comparison, i.e., that equating bandwidth magnitudes across these physically different systems is a useful indication that similar correlation-relevant energy scales may be present despite differences in microscopic origin, symmetry, and interaction screening between the two systems.
